title: Standard electrode potentials E°, standard cell potentials Ecell and the Nernst equation
summary: 9701 A Level electrochemistry — E° tables, cell emf, ΔG, and the Nernst equation with PhET circuit kit.
learningObjectives: Use standard electrode potential data to predict cell reactions.
Calculate E°cell and link to ΔG°.
Apply the Nernst equation at non-standard concentrations.

simpleExplanation.title: Measuring reducing power
simpleExplanation.summary: E° measures tendency to be reduced. The more positive E° is reduced at the cathode; the cell emf is E°(cathode) − E°(anode).
steps: SHE defined as E° = 0.00 V — all E° relative to H⁺/H₂.
Nernst at 298 K: E = E° − (0.059/n) log Q.

sections.content: **Electrode potentials** (24.2) follow **electrolysis** (24.1). Practise **cell diagrams** (single line, phases) and **Nernst** with concentration changes.
